pi_items.source: Task.json metadata and export_result.sh confirm this path
Web search confirms southwest (225°) is the prevailing wind direction in the Solent area. Task description explicitly states 'South-West (225 degrees)'
Physical principle: wind from bearing 225° creates drift in opposite direction (225° + 180° = 405° = 45°). Task description confirms 'Northeast, ~045 degrees'
Task-specific tolerance parameter. Verifier.py line 186-189 uses this tolerance to accept bearing within ±20° of target
Web search (NOAA) defines gale force as 34-47 knots. Task description specifies 'between 35 and 45 knots'
Web search (NOAA) defines gale force as 34-47 knots. Task description specifies 'between 35 and 45 knots'
Task description and verifier.py (line 177) both specify 0.5 knots as minimum

CONTRADICTION DETECTED: Task description (line 5) states 'slow speed (0.5 to 1.5 knots)', but verifier.py line 177 checks '0.5 <= speed <= 2.0' and metadata claims max of 2.0. The actual validator accepts 2.0 knots, contradicting the 1.5 knot limit stated in the task description.
Path verified in setup_task.sh (line 6) and export_result.sh (line 6). Environment setup confirmed in env.json (user 'ga' created)

privileged_info_summary: The anchor dragging detection task uses synthetic simulation data created in Bridge Command. The scenario is set in the real St Helens Anchorage area (Solent, Isle of Wight, approximately 50.696°N, 1.112°W). Wind configuration uses 225° (Southwest), the verified prevailing wind direction for this region during gales (verified as 34-47 knots per NOAA). The target drag bearing of 45° (Northeast) is physically correct as the opposite drift vector from 225° winds. Wind speed bounds of 35-45 knots are verified as gale force per maritime standards. A critical contradiction exists: the task description specifies dragging vessel speed as 0.5 to 1.5 knots, but the verifier accepts 0.5 to 2.0 knots maximum, creating ambiguity in the success criteria.
